name: Jollof Rice (West African Paella)
recipeIngredient: 1 Sprig of thyme, crushed, or 1 tsp dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Add in as many kinds of meat or vegetables as you like or can afford into this dish. Shrimp makes an extravagant but wonderful addition.
2. Use a heavy pot or large cast iron skillet to discourage burning.
3. Stirring often is the key to success with this dish. Use a large wooden spoon.
4. Cover the pot tightly.
5. Skin, debone and chop the chicken into 1 inch square pieces.
6. In a heavy pot, brown the chicken in oil over medium heat.
7. Add onions and peppers. Cook for 5 to 10 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, saute the shrimp in a small amount of oil until about half done (5 minutes or so).
9. Drain the vegetables and add them to the chicken pot along with the shrimp, tomatoes, salt, pepper, and thyme.
10. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
11. Combine rice with tomato paste, which should coat the rice grains without drowning them. The finished dish should have an orange tint; too much tomato paste will make it red.
12. Stir the coated rice into the pot and continue to simmer, adding water sparingly as needed, until the meat, rice, and vegetables are tender. Serve.
